@@ -378,7 +378,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-y6z","title":"Create BrennerQuote component with beautiful styling","description":"# Task Create a stunning quote component specifically for Brenner quotes. # Why Special Treatment Brenner's quotes are the crown jewels of this project. They deserve more than gray italic text. Each quote should feel like wisdom worth savoring. # Design ``` ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ❝ │ │ Exclusion is always a tremendously good │ │ thing in science. │ │ │ │ — Sydney Brenner, §42 │ │ [📋] [🔗] │ └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` # Visual Elements 1. **Opening quote mark** - Large, decorative, in primary color (faded) 2. **Quote text** - Larger font, good line height, slightly italic 3. **Gradient border** - Left border with primary→accent gradient 4. **Background** - Subtle card background with glassmorphism 5. **Attribution** - Right-aligned, smaller, with § link 6. **Action buttons** - Copy, Share (appear on hover) # Animation - Fade + slide in on scroll reveal - Subtle glow on hover - Button reveal on hover # Props ```tsx interface BrennerQuoteProps { children: React.ReactNode; source?: string; // e.g., '§42' or 'Interview, 1998' sourceUrl?: string; // Link to transcript section variant?: 'default' | 'featured' | 'inline'; } ``` # Variants - **default** - Standard quote block - **featured** - Larger, more prominent (for landing page) - **inline** - Compact, for use within paragraphs # Accessibility - Proper `
` semantics - `` for attribution - Focus states on buttons - Screen reader announces as quote # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Quote renders with all visual elements - [ ] Animation on scroll reveal - [ ] Copy button works - [ ] Share button opens share dialog - [ ] § link navigates correctly - [ ] All three variants work - [ ] Accessible markup","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:53.472975562-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.423890236-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.423890236-05:00","close_reason":"Folded into landing quote presentation (brenner_bot-9o2)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-y6z","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.812013867-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-y6z","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-2n1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.847436402-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-ypz","title":"CLI: add inbox fetch + thread summary helpers","description":"Extend brenner.ts with Agent Mail helpers: fetch inbox, read thread, summarize thread, ack messages, and basic polling.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T17:22:03.668949665-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T18:34:41.881677878-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T18:34:41.881677878-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.5.2 (CLI mail tooling)"} {"id":"brenner_bot-ysc","title":"Task: Implement swipe navigation between documents","description":"Add gesture-based navigation between documents on mobile. ## Purpose Swipe navigation is a natural mobile pattern. Users expect to swipe left/right to go between items. ## Scope - Swipe LEFT to go to NEXT document - Swipe RIGHT to go to PREVIOUS document - Only on document pages (/corpus/[doc]) - Threshold: 50% of screen width or 100px, whichever is less ## Technical Approach ### Option A: CSS Scroll Snap - Wrap prev/current/next in scroll container - Use scroll-snap-type for snapping - Detect scroll end for navigation ### Option B: Touch Event Handling - Listen for touchstart/touchmove/touchend - Calculate swipe distance and velocity - Trigger navigation if threshold met ### Option C: Library (react-swipeable) - Use existing gesture library - Less code, more reliable - Small bundle impact (~3KB) Recommended: Option C for reliability, unless bundle size is critical. ## Visual Feedback - Show edge peek of prev/next during swipe - Elastic bounce at first/last document - Cancel swipe if released before threshold ## Accessibility - Swipe is enhancement, not requirement - Prev/Next buttons always visible - Works without gesture support ## Files to Modify - Create gesture wrapper component - Wrap document page content - Handle navigation events ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Swipe left/right navigates documents - [ ] Visual feedback during swipe - [ ] Threshold prevents accidental navigation - [ ] Edge behavior at first/last doc - [ ] Doesn't interfere with content scrolling - [ ] Prev/Next buttons still work","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:13.330351376-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T07:21:30.339121607-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T07:21:30.339121607-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented swipe navigation on /corpus/[doc]","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-ysc","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0dk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:24.946600519-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-yz5","title":"Create Bayesian mapping visualization","description":"# Task Build an interactive visualization of the implicit Bayesianism table from the README. # The Table | Brenner Move | Bayesian Operation | |--------------|-------------------| | Enumerate 3+ models | Maintain explicit prior distribution | | Hunt paradoxes | Find high-probability contradictions | | Third alternative | Reserve probability mass for misspecification | | Design forbidden patterns | Maximize expected information gain | | Seven-cycle log paper | Choose experiments with extreme likelihood ratios | | ... | ... | # Visualization Options 1. **Interactive table** - Hover for explanations, click for examples 2. **Animated flow** - Show the Brenner move flowing to Bayesian concept 3. **Card pairs** - Brenner move card links to Bayesian card # The Objective Function Display prominently: ``` Expected Information Gain × Downstream Leverage Score(E) = ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Time × Cost × Ambiguity × Infrastructure-Dependence ``` With explanation of what Brenner optimized (small denominator, large numerator). # Design - Clean table with subtle row hover - Brenner moves in one color, Bayesian in another - Expandable rows for full explanations - Links to transcript quotes where applicable # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Table renders all 12 mappings - [ ] Interactive hover/expand - [ ] Objective function displayed - [ ] Links to sources - [ ] Responsive layout","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:05.719598117-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:42:10.688180964-05:00","labels":["method","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-yz5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:25.053470213-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-yz5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-twy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:28:08.431879875-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-yz5","title":"Create Bayesian mapping visualization","description":"# Task Build an interactive visualization of the implicit Bayesianism table from the README. # The Table | Brenner Move | Bayesian Operation | |--------------|-------------------| | Enumerate 3+ models | Maintain explicit prior distribution | | Hunt paradoxes | Find high-probability contradictions | | Third alternative | Reserve probability mass for misspecification | | Design forbidden patterns | Maximize expected information gain | | Seven-cycle log paper | Choose experiments with extreme likelihood ratios | | ... | ... | # Visualization Options 1. **Interactive table** - Hover for explanations, click for examples 2. **Animated flow** - Show the Brenner move flowing to Bayesian concept 3. **Card pairs** - Brenner move card links to Bayesian card # The Objective Function Display prominently: ``` Expected Information Gain × Downstream Leverage Score(E) = ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Time × Cost × Ambiguity × Infrastructure-Dependence ``` With explanation of what Brenner optimized (small denominator, large numerator). # Design - Clean table with subtle row hover - Brenner moves in one color, Bayesian in another - Expandable rows for full explanations - Links to transcript quotes where applicable # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Table renders all 12 mappings - [ ] Interactive hover/expand - [ ] Objective function displayed - [ ] Links to sources - [ ] Responsive layout","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:05.719598117-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:45:51.050959453-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T13:45:51.050959453-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented interactive Bayesian crosswalk with 12 mappings, objective function display, and transcript anchor links","labels":["method","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-yz5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:25.053470213-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-yz5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-twy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:28:08.431879875-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-z9y","title":"Task: Document mobile UI issues","description":"Review screenshots and catalog all mobile UI issues. ## Purpose Systematic documentation of problems before fixing. This prevents ad-hoc fixes that miss issues. ## Issue Categories to Look For ### Layout Issues - Content overflow (horizontal scroll) - Improper stacking - Elements too close to edges - Safe area violations (notch, home indicator) ### Spacing Issues - Inconsistent padding/margins - Elements too crowded - Breathing room for touch targets ### Typography Issues - Text too small to read - Lines too long (> 65 characters) - Truncation problems - Poor hierarchy ### Interactive Element Issues - Touch targets < 44px - Elements too close together - Hidden/inaccessible controls - Unclear tap states ### Visual Issues - Alignment problems - Inconsistent styling - Dark mode contrast issues - Animation/transition problems ## Deliverable Markdown document with: - Screenshot reference - Issue description - Severity (P0 blocking, P1 high, P2 medium, P3 low) - Suggested fix ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All screenshots reviewed - [ ] Issues categorized by type - [ ] Severity assigned to each - [ ] Document committed to repo (e.g., docs/mobile-audit.md)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:59.387282661-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T02:13:29.405100227-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T02:13:29.405100227-05:00","close_reason":"Mobile audit document complete at apps/web/docs/mobile-audit-2025-12-30.md. All pages screenshotted (30+), issues categorized with severity ratings, and document committed.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-z9y","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-3vw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:28:36.551894446-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-z9y","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-95h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:28:36.647631924-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-zos","title":"TanStack Table: Upgrade CrosswalkTable with Sorting","description":"# Goal Upgrade the CrosswalkTable component to use TanStack Table for proper sorting, filtering, and accessibility. ## Background CrosswalkTable shows a comparison matrix: - Rows: 5 concepts (Problem Selection, Hypothesis Generation, etc.) - Columns: 3 models (Opus, GPT, Gemini) - Cells: Each model's interpretation of the concept Current implementation is a simple map with hover states: ```typescript const CROSSWALK_DATA: CrosswalkRow[] = [ { concept: '...', opus: '...', gpt: '...', gemini: '...' }, // ... 5 rows ]; {CROSSWALK_DATA.map((row, i) => ( setHoveredRow(i)}> {row.concept} {row.opus} {row.gpt} {row.gemini} ))} ``` ## Why TanStack Table? Even for a small table, Table provides: 1. **Sorting**: Click column headers to sort 2. **Column visibility**: Toggle which models to show 3. **Accessibility**: Proper ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation 4. **Future-proof**: Easy to add filtering, pagination later ## Implementation ### 1. Define columns ```typescript const columns = useMemo(() => [ columnHelper.accessor('concept', { header: 'Concept', cell: info => {info.getValue()}, }), columnHelper.accessor('opus', { header: () => , cell: info => info.getValue(), }), columnHelper.accessor('gpt', { header: () => , cell: info => info.getValue(), }), columnHelper.accessor('gemini', { header: () => , cell: info => info.getValue(), }), ], []); ``` ### 2. Create table instance ```typescript const table = useReactTable({ data: CROSSWALK_DATA, columns, getCoreRowModel: getCoreRowModel(), getSortedRowModel: getSortedRowModel(), state: { sorting }, onSortingChange: setSorting, }); ``` ### 3. Render with Table primitives ```typescript {table.getHeaderGroups().map(headerGroup => ( {headerGroup.headers.map(header => ( ))} ))} {table.getRowModel().rows.map(row => ( {row.getVisibleCells().map(cell => ( ))} ))}
{flexRender(header.column.columnDef.header, header.getContext())} {{ asc: ' ↑', desc: ' ↓', }[header.column.getIsSorted() as string] ?? null}
{flexRender(cell.column.columnDef.cell, cell.getContext())}
``` ## Enhancements Over Current ### Sorting Click any column header to sort: - Alphabetical by concept - Alphabetical by model interpretation ### Column Visibility (Optional) Add toggles to show/hide specific models. Useful for focused comparison. ### Cell Click to Navigate Make cells clickable to navigate to that model's full distillation. ### Keyboard Navigation - Arrow keys to move between cells - Enter to activate cell link - Tab for focus management ## Considerations ### Styling Keep the existing model-specific color coding. Table primitives are unstyled - apply existing Tailwind classes. ### Mobile On mobile, table may need horizontal scroll. Consider: Card view on mobile, table on desktop. ### Row Hover Maintain existing row hover highlight. Table provides row.getIsSelected() etc. for states. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Table uses TanStack Table - [ ] Column sorting works - [ ] Keyboard navigation works - [ ] Existing styling preserved - [ ] Model badges in headers - [ ] Cell links to distillations work ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/components/distillation/CrosswalkTable.tsx","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:18:45.917373474-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:55:45.850919825-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:55:45.850919825-05:00","close_reason":"Upgraded CrosswalkTable to use TanStack Table with column sorting, keyboard navigation (Enter/Space), ARIA attributes (role=grid, aria-sort), and sort indicators. Build passes. Preserved existing styling and tooltips.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-zos","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1uu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:19:20.347689225-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-zsq","title":"Quote bank: normalize operator mapping + tags","description":"Normalize quote bank into operator-keyed mapping (⊘/𝓛/⧉/≡/✂/⟂/↑/⇓/ΔE/∿/⊙) with a canonical tag taxonomy; keep file sprawl minimal and ensure every operator has ≥3 grounded quotes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:00:57.677703996-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T18:34:41.852618607-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T18:34:41.852618607-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.2.3 (Quote bank normalization)"}
